test_description: For each masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted map, set masked pixels to zero and compute pseudo-power spectra \tilde C_\ell for 2 <= \ell <= 30. From the 1000 LCDM simulations, compute the ensemble mean pseudo-spectrum \bar C_\ell using the identical pipeline. For each map form whitened low-l powers r_\ell = \tilde C_\ell / \bar C_\ell, normalize them as p_\ell = r_\ell / \sum_{\ell=2}^{30} r_\ell, and return the normalized Shannon entropy H = -\sum_{\ell=2}^{30} p_\ell \ln p_\ell / \ln(29). This single scalar is low when the large-angle power is unusually concentrated in a few multipoles and high when it is unusually evenly spread.
test_hypothesis: The Planck low-\(\ell\) pseudo-bandpower distribution, after whitening by the LCDM simulation mean, has an anomalous entropy compared with LCDM simulations, indicating that power is either unusually concentrated in a few multipoles or unusually evenly spread across multipoles.
justificaton: The statistic is explicitly interpretable in both directions: low \(H\) indicates unusually concentrated low-\(\ell\) power, while high \(H\) indicates unusually even spreading. The code computes lower-tail, upper-tail, and two-sided empirical p-values and does not impose a single directional prediction. Therefore the appropriate default is a two-tailed anomaly test.

custom_summary.test_description: Whitened Low-l Bandpower Entropy: fit and subtract the monopole and dipole on unmasked pixels, set masked pixels to zero, compute pseudo-C_ell for 2<=ell<=30, whiten by the LCDM simulation mean pseudo-spectrum, normalize the whitened powers, and return H=-sum p_ell ln p_ell / ln(29).
custom_summary.statistic: Normalized Shannon entropy of p_ell proportional to tilde C_ell/bar C_ell for 2<=ell<=30.
custom_summary.whitening_reference: mean pseudo-C_ell over the supplied LCDM simulation spectra cached from analyze_map
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: lower tail = whitened low-l power concentrated in unusually few multipoles; upper tail = unusually even spread
custom_summary.masked_pixel_handling: finite pixels are used for the monopole/dipole fit; np.nan mask pixels are then filled with 0.0 before healpy.anafast

plot_spec.title: Whitened low-l bandpower entropy
plot_spec.caption: Distribution of normalized entropy for the 2<=ell<=30 whitened pseudo-bandpowers. Low H indicates concentration into a few multipoles.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in either 5% tail of the simulation distribution for this entropy statistic.

test_summary.Description: Masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted Planck and LCDM simulation maps were converted to pseudo-\(C_\ell\) spectra for \(2\le\ell\le30\), whitened by the simulation-mean pseudo-spectrum, normalized across multipoles, and summarized by normalized Shannon entropy \(H\). Low \(H\) means power concentrated in few multipoles; high \(H\) means unusually even spreading.
test_summary.Results: Planck has \(H\simeq0.987\)–0.988, close to the simulation mean/median \(\simeq0.986\)–0.987 with simulation scatter \(\simeq0.005\). Its percentile is about 58% low-to-high, with two-tailed empirical \(p\simeq0.84\)–0.85 and \(|z|<0.4\).
test_summary.Interpretation: Planck is not in either the low-entropy or high-entropy tail. The whitened low-\(\ell\) power is neither unusually concentrated in a few multipoles nor unusually evenly distributed compared with LCDM simulations.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: General Planck low-\(\ell\) anomalies are widely discussed, e.g. Planck Collaboration VII, A&A 641, A7 (2020), but previous searches found no directly comparable published use of this specific whitened low-\(\ell\) bandpower Shannon-entropy statistic.
